name: Type-Safe Data Pipeline Library
scenario: Build a small but real library of generic utility classes that a team would actually use. Every class is generic — works for any type. This is what writing reusable code as a library author looks like.
description: A collection of four generic utility classes: Pair<A,B>, Result<T>, Page<T>, and EventBus<E>. Each solves a real problem generically. Together they form a mini utility library demonstrating all key generic class concepts.

core.description: `Pair<A, B>` — immutable, getFirst(), getSecond(), swap() → Pair<B,A>, equals()/hashCode() using both fields, toString().
`Result<T>` — success(T)/failure(String) factory methods, isSuccess(), getValue(), getError(), orElse(T), map(Function<T,R>) → Result<R>, toString().
`Page<T>` — content (unmodifiable list), pageNumber (1-based), pageSize, totalElements, totalPages(), hasNext(), hasPrevious(), isEmpty(), toString().
main() demonstrates all four classes working together: Result for an operation, Pair for key-value return, Page for paginated results, EventBus for notifications. All without casts.
Each class has a Javadoc-style comment (/** ... */) explaining the type parameter meaning.

bonus.description: Add `Either<L, R>` — left or right value (L=error type, R=success type). Like Result but both types are generic. `left(L)`, `right(R)`, `isLeft()`, `isRight()`, `mapRight(Function<R,R2>)`, `fold(Function<L,T>, Function<R,T>)` → T.
Add `Memoize<T>` — wraps a `Supplier<T>` and caches the result after first call. `get()` calls the supplier once, then returns cached value. Demonstrates Supplier lazy evaluation in generic context.
Add `batch(List<T> items, int batchSize)` static method (in a utility class) that returns `List<List<T>>` — split into batches of given size. Generic, works for any list type.

data_to_use:
Demonstration in main():
- Result: parse payment amount '5000' → success; 'abc' → failure
- Pair: user ID 'U001' + account balance 50000.0
- Page: 7 product names, page 1 of 3, 3 per page, 7 total
- EventBus<String>: 2 subscribers, publish 2 events
example_output:
=== Result ===
Parsed 5000: Success(5000.0)
Parsed abc: Failure(Not a number: abc)
Formatted: ₹5000.00

=== Pair ===
(U001, 50000.0)
Swapped: (50000.0, U001)

=== Page ===
Page 1/3 (7 total, 3 per page)
[Mouse, Keyboard, USB Hub]
Has next: true | Has prev: false

=== EventBus ===
Handler1: order placed
Handler2: order placed
Handler1: payment done
Handler2: payment done

criteria: Zero casts in client code (15 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. The challenge: every class must be fully generic with no Object casts in client code. Result.map() return type must be `<R> Result<R>`. Page requires defensive copy of content list. EventBus.unsubscribe() requires reference equality (same Consumer instance).
submission_instructions: Save as DataPipelineLib.java. Push to GitHub — this is a reusable library. Comment: 'Generic utility library — no casts in client code.'
tips_for_success: Result.map(): `public <R> Result<R> map(Function<T, R> fn)` — its own type parameter R
EventBus.unsubscribe: `listeners.remove(listener)` uses reference equality — caller must pass the same Consumer reference
Zero casts means: never write `(SomeType)` anywhere in the calling code
concepts_reinforced: Type parameters on methods vs class
No casts in client code = core generic benefit
Type parameter naming conventions with Javadoc
